## Web UX Guidelines

Accessibility, keyboard navigation, responsive design, dark mode, and inclusive interaction patterns.

### Quick Reference

- WCAG 2.1 AA compliance is the minimum bar.
- Semantic HTML first — ARIA only when native elements fall short.
- Keyboard-navigable: every interactive element reachable via Tab/Enter/Space.
- Dark mode via CSS custom properties — no separate stylesheets.
- Mobile-first responsive with `min-width` breakpoints.
- Minimum 44×44px touch target for all interactive elements.
- Color is never the only indicator of state.
- Focus indicators must always be visible.

### Applying the Rules

**Step 1: Identify the surface.**
Confirm the file is UI-facing (component, stylesheet, or layout). Server-only files do not need these rules applied.

**Step 2: Apply the full rule set.**
Read `references/rules-detail.md` for the complete rule set with rationale and examples.

**Step 3: Verify accessibility.**
Run automated checks (axe, Lighthouse) and manually tab through every interactive element. Test with a screen reader for any feature that introduces new interactive patterns or dynamic content.
